mnemonics: CAP: Cartesian Product is All Possible ordered pairs from two sets A and B. Remember 'All Pairs' from 'CAP'.
keyPoints: A relation from set A to set B is a subset of the Cartesian product A × B.
The Cartesian product A × B is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a ∈ A and b ∈ B.
A function is a special type of relation where each element in the domain (A) is related to exactly one element in the codomain (B).
commonMistakes: Confusing the order in an ordered pair: (a, b) ≠ (b, a) unless a = b.
Assuming a relation from A to B must include every element of A and B, but it is only a subset of A × B.
Mistaking a relation for a function by not ensuring each input has exactly one output.
